meta.title: Leo Gonsalves — Software engineer
meta.description: Engineer working in React, C# and more. Available for contract and consulting work.

headlines: Programmer with design instincts — drawn to domains that fight back.
Half a decade of React and C# spent in systems with too many moving parts.

about.title: A messy problem is my favorite kind of problem.
bodies:
Half a decade spent cooking React and C# code, with a soft spot for slightly absurd problem statements. So far that's meant clinical-trial supply chains, satellite carbon analysis, and Belgian payroll.
Outside work, I build games, interactive media, tools, and LLM-based experiments. They all serve the same purpose: finding new corners of the tools I use professionally.

I'm a programmer with a designer's habits, working mostly in React and C#. Half a decade in, my taste runs toward systems where “this should be simple” turns out to be the most expensive sentence in the room — clinical trials, carbon registries, Belgian payroll.
Outside hours are spent making games, creating interactive media, tools, and building LLM-based experiments – making sure I never get too comfortable with the tools I use professionally

Half a decade of React and C# spent inside slightly absurd domains — clinical-trial supply, satellite carbon analysis, cafeteria-plan driven payroll. I'm not picky about the stack so much as the shape of the problem: dense, under-specified, occasionally regulated into a corner.
Outside work I build games, interactive media, tools, and LLM-based experiments. A finished project is the cleanest proof I know that I still enjoy this.

I'm a programmer with a designer's habits, working mostly in React and C#, and have a soft spot for slightly absurd problem statements. I never say no to a challenge. So far that's meant clinical-trial supply chains, satellite carbon analysis, and Belgian payroll.
Creating fun, smart, and usable experiences is what keeps me going. Outside work, I build games, interactive media, tools, and LLM-based experiments. They all serve the same purpose: finding new corners of the tools I use professionally.

titles: Three companies. Three problem statements that didn't fit on a slide.
Three companies, three regulated industries, one stubborn engineer.
work.lede: Shipped under audit, under deadline, and occasionally under satellite passes.

n-side.contribution: Software engineer on the team that built Lighthouse — a clinical-trial supply-planning platform modernizing a legacy Java stack.

tris.contribution: Part of the founding team behind a bootstrapped geospatial analysis startup, I built a carbon-credit access and analysis tool to Brazilian farmers and landowners.

payflip.contribution: As senior software engineer, I’m cCurrently building unified card-payment infrastructure to revolutionize Belgian cafeteria-plan benefits and promote salary transparency.

side.title: Smaller things, built for the joy of finishing them.
side.lede: Outside work, mostly weekends. A different register — same instinct.

recolonizer.title: Recolonizer
recolonizer.blurb: A tower defense built solo in Unity — pathing, waves, and a stubborn fondness for C#.

glutton-for-gluten.title: Glutton for Gluten
glutton-for-gluten.blurb: Game-jam platformer; built in a weekend and still walks.

cowboy-investor.title: Cowboy Investor
cowboy-investor.blurb: A small LLM-backed wrapper that attempts to give personalized investment advice.

contact.title: Have a system that's grown teeth?
contact.body: Taking on contract and consulting work — React and C# preferred, ambiguous problem statements welcome.

contact_form.meta_description: Get in touch about a project, a technical question, or just to say hi.
contact_form.title: Get in touch
contact_form.opener: Open to consultancy, contract work, and building your project end-to-end.
contact_form.success_body: Thank you for reaching out. I'll get back to you as soon as I can.
contact_form.error_generic: Something went wrong — try again or email me directly.
contact_form.error_expired: This form expired. Refresh the page and try again.

validation.email_invalid: That doesn't look like an email address

templates.quote:
Hi — I'd like to discuss a project. Here's the rough shape:

- What it is:
- What needs building (high level):
- Timeline I'm hoping for:
- Budget range (or 'still figuring this out'):
- Anything I'm uncertain about:

Happy to share more on a call.
templates.games: Hi — I came across [your game / project] and wanted to reach out about [collab / question / something else]. A bit about me:
templates.technical:
Hi — I'm trying to [what you're building or doing]. Specifically, I'm stuck on:

What I've tried so far:

Any pointers welcome.
templates.meeting: Hi — I'd like to book a call to discuss [topic]. My timezone is [tz] and I'm generally free [days/times]. Roughly [duration] should be enough.

projects.meta_description: Selected professional and personal projects: Lighthouse at N-SIDE, TRIS, Payflip, and a handful of weekend things.
projects.title: Professional and personal work
projects.lede: From regulated industries to weekend jams, a selection of projects big and small that I've found myself drawn to over the years.
projects.professional_summary: Founding-team or early-engineer work in regulated industries — clinical supply, carbon, payroll.
projects.personal_summary: Weekends, jams, and small experiments. Built for fun to bring joy to others.

n-side.excerpt: Founding-team engineer on Lighthouse, modernizing N-SIDE's legacy Java stack into a new clinical trial supply planning platform.

tris.excerpt: Bootstrapped a geospatial analysis tool democratizing carbon-credit access for Brazilian farmers and landowners.

payflip.excerpt: Building unified card payment infrastructure for Belgian cafeteria-plan benefits and salary transparency.

glutton-for-gluten.excerpt: Platformer built during a game jam.

cowboy-investor.excerpt: An LLM wrapper offering opinionated, experimental investment advice.
